- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""I-wire-001 W5 — credibility LLM-tiering winner (PG_CREDIBILITY_LLM_TIERING).
2	
3	The bake-off winner (`scripts/dr_benchmark/upstream_bakeoff/credibility_winner.json`):
4	an LLM (GLM-5.2 via OpenRouter) classifies a source DIRECTLY into the POLARIS T1-T7
5	tier scheme from the observable authority payload, beating the deterministic 22-rule
6	floor by +0.353 macro-F1 on the 27-row GATE-0-validated real clinical tier gold. The
7	headline repair is the rule-floor's two EXPOSED-weak tiers:
8	  * T7 social / predatory-OA / abstract-stub: 0.000 -> 1.000 (there is no social->T7
9	    rule; the floor classifies social platforms T6 via RP1_social_platform_early).
10	  * T2 evidence-synthesis / guideline under-recall: 0.400 -> 0.889.
11	No tier regresses.
12	
13	ARCHITECTURE (mirrors `authority/credibility_judge_caller.py`):
14	  * The LLM call is DEPENDENCY-INJECTED (`call_llm(prompt) -> text`) so the prompt
15	    build + JSON parse are pure and offline-testable; the production caller binds
16	    GLM-5.2 + family-segregation + provider-pin + budget via the existing
17	    `make_openrouter_credibility_caller` factory.
18	  * Tiering is a per-citation WEIGHT, NEVER a drop (CLAUDE.md §-1.3). On ANY judge
19	    error / timeout / malformed output, the per-source result falls back to the
20	    deterministic rules-floor — instant, no source is ever dropped.
21	  * The faithfulness engine (strict_verify / NLI / 4-role / provenance) is FROZEN and
22	    untouched. This module only changes the per-source tier WEIGHT.
23	
24	BOUNDED PARALLELISM (operator mandate, §8 of the wiring plan): per-SOURCE LLM tiering
25	runs bounded-parallel via a `ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=PG_TIER_LLM_WORKERS)`,
26	order-independent (gather-then-sort by source index). The rules-floor is computed for
27	every source first (the instant deterministic fallback) so a slow/failed LLM call never
28	blocks or drops a source.
29	
30	Default-OFF: `classify_source_tier` only routes here when PG_CREDIBILITY_LLM_TIERING is
31	set to a truthy value; otherwise the legacy rule body runs byte-identical.
32	"""
33	from __future__ import annotations
34	
35	import dataclasses
36	import json
37	import logging
38	import os
39	import re
40	import time
41	from concurrent.futures import FIRST_COMPLETED, ThreadPoolExecutor, wait as futures_wait
42	from typing import Callable
43	
44	from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.tier_classifier import (
45	    STATISTICAL_AGENCY_DOMAINS,
46	    T7_STUB_CONTENT_CHARS,
47	    ClassificationResult,
48	    ClassificationSignals,
49	    TierLevel,
50	    _classify_source_tier_rules,
51	    _detect_retraction_marker,
52	    _domain_matches,
53	    _is_corroborated_scholarly_venue,
54	    _normalize_domain,
55	)
56	
57	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
58	
59	# Bounded-parallel cap for the per-source LLM tiering fan-out (LAW VI). Default 10
60	# mirrors the wiring plan §8.2 (`PG_TIER_LLM_WORKERS=10`). Clamped >=1 so a misconfig
61	# can never produce a zero/negative worker count.
62	_ENV_TIER_LLM_WORKERS = "PG_TIER_LLM_WORKERS"
63	_DEFAULT_TIER_LLM_WORKERS = 10
64	
65	# Per-source LLM-tiering outcome states, surfaced as REAL runtime counts by the
66	# post-execution canary in ``classify_sources_llm_tiering`` (never a config echo).
67	# SUCCESS = GLM returned a valid tier; FALLBACK = llm_tier_one returned None
68	# (judge_error / malformed / retracted) so the deterministic rules-floor was kept;
69	# ERROR = an unexpected exception escaped llm_tier_one (contracted not to raise).
70	_TIER_STATUS_SUCCESS = "success"
71	_TIER_STATUS_FALLBACK = "fallback"
72	_TIER_STATUS_ERROR = "error"
73	
74	# I-deepfix-001 D5 (#1344): HONEST machine-readable batch tiering MODE surfaced on the
75	# returned result (the §-1.3 "completes-not-claims" fix). On a mirror blank-200 / trickle
76	# storm the GLM tiering can degrade to the rules-floor for EVERY source (llm_success == 0);
77	# the run MUST report that as ``rules_floor_degraded``, NEVER as ``tiered_via_glm``.
78	# Credibility is a WEIGHT (T1-T7), never a hard gate — so the run CONTINUES; the degrade is
79	# DISCLOSED (a LOUD warning + this machine-readable status), never silent, never a false
80	# "tiered_via_glm" claim (the prior false-positive this fix kills).
81	_TIERING_MODE_TIERED_VIA_GLM = "tiered_via_glm"   # GLM tiered EVERY source (llm_success == total)
82	_TIERING_MODE_PARTIAL = "partial"                 # GLM tiered SOME (0 < llm_success < total)
83	_TIERING_MODE_RULES_FLOOR_DEGRADED = "rules_floor_degraded"  # GLM tiered NONE (llm_success == 0, total > 0)
84	_VALID_TIERING_MODES = frozenset({
85	    _TIERING_MODE_TIERED_VIA_GLM,
86	    _TIERING_MODE_PARTIAL,
87	    _TIERING_MODE_RULES_FLOOR_DEGRADED,
88	})
89	
90	# Valid tier labels the LLM may return (UNKNOWN excluded — the LLM must commit to a
91	# tier; an unparseable / out-of-scheme answer falls back to the rules-floor).
92	_VALID_TIER_LABELS = {"T1", "T2", "T3", "T4", "T5", "T6", "T7"}
93	
94	# The POLARIS T1-T7 scheme, transcribed VERBATIM from the rules-floor's own documented
95	# scheme (tier_classifier.py class docstring + domain frozensets), per the winning
96	# scorecard's `rubric` field. NO answer-row leakage — this is the floor's own scheme.
97	_TIER_SCHEME = (
98	    "T1 = peer-reviewed primary study (RCT, prospective cohort, case-control, "
99	    "cross-sectional, lab/mechanistic study in a peer-reviewed journal).\n"
100	    "T2 = peer-reviewed evidence synthesis or clinical guideline (systematic review, "
101	    "meta-analysis, Cochrane review, NICE/ADA/specialty-society guideline).\n"
102	    "T3 = government / regulatory body (FDA, EMA, NICE-as-regulator, WHO, CDC, "
103	    "Health Canada, national regulator).\n"
104	    "T4 = peer-reviewed narrative review, commentary, editorial, perspective, preprint, "
105	    "or repository deposit (not peer-reviewed primary research).\n"
106	    "T5 = industry-funded report (pharmaceutical-company HCP portal, manufacturer drug "
107	    "monograph, sponsored brand site, paid market-research / consulting collateral).\n"
108	    "T6 = mainstream news, blog, or non-peer-reviewed consumer-health web content.\n"
109	    "T7 = social-media / user-generated content (YouTube, Reddit, Facebook, X, "
110	    "Instagram, forums), predatory open-access, or abstract-only / conference-abstract / "
111	    "stub with no full article."
112	)
113	
114	_PROMPT_HEAD = (
115	    "You are a source-credibility TIER classifier for ONE retrieved source. Assign the "
116	    "single best-fitting POLARIS tier (T1..T7) from the observable signals below. Judge "
117	    "the SOURCE TYPE / venue authority, not the topic.\n\n"
118	    "TIER SCHEME:\n{scheme}\n\n"
119	    "SOURCE:\n"
120	    "  url: {url}\n"
121	    "  title: {title}\n"
122	    "  publication_type: {pub_type}\n"
123	    "  source_type: {source_type}\n"
124	    "  venue: {venue}\n"
125	    "  is_retracted: {is_retracted}\n"
126	    "  fetched_content_length: {content_length}\n\n"
127	)
128	# I-deepfix-002 (#1363): the venue-corroboration prompt hardening is gated by the SAME
129	# kill-switch as the cap (PG_TIER_REQUIRE_VENUE_CORROBORATION). With the switch OFF the
130	# prompt is byte-identical to the legacy un-hardened prompt (no prompt drift on revert).
131	_PROMPT_VENUE_HARDENING = (
132	    "T1 and T2 REQUIRE a NAMED peer-reviewed venue or recognized publisher. Do NOT "
133	    "infer T1/T2 from a DOI, a URL, or an academic-sounding title alone. If venue and "
134	    "source_type are empty, or the venue is unrecognized/obscure, classify as T4 "
135	    "(peer-reviewed but unverified venue) or lower.\n\n"
136	)
137	_PROMPT_TAIL = (
138	    "Return STRICT JSON only, no prose, no code fence:\n"
139	    '{{"tier": "<one of T1,T2,T3,T4,T5,T6,T7>", '
140	    '"rationale": "<one short sentence citing the signal you relied on>"}}'
141	)
142	
143	
144	def build_tier_prompt(signals: ClassificationSignals) -> str:
145	    """Pure: render the per-source LLM tiering prompt from observable signals only.
146	
147	    Carries ONLY observable fields (url/title/pub_type/source_type/venue/is_retracted/
148	    content_length) — never any gold tier or rule verdict (LAW II, no answer leakage).
149	    The B2 venue-corroboration hardening clause is included only when the kill-switch is
150	    ON; OFF yields the byte-identical legacy prompt.
151	    """
152	    _hardening = _PROMPT_VENUE_HARDENING if _venue_corroboration_required() else ""
153	    return (_PROMPT_HEAD + _hardening + _PROMPT_TAIL).format(
154	        scheme=_TIER_SCHEME,
155	        url=signals.url or "",
156	        title=signals.title or "",
157	        pub_type=signals.openalex_publication_type or "",
158	        source_type=signals.openalex_source_type or "",
159	        venue=signals.openalex_venue or "",
160	        is_retracted=bool(signals.openalex_is_retracted),
161	        content_length=signals.fetched_content_length or 0,
162	    )
163	
164	
165	def parse_tier_response(text: str) -> tuple[TierLevel | None, str]:
166	    """Pure: parse the LLM JSON response into a TierLevel + rationale.
167	
168	    Returns ``(None, "")`` on ANY malformed / out-of-scheme output (the caller then
169	    falls back to the rules-floor — fail-honest, never fabricate a tier). Tolerates a
170	    stray code fence by extracting the first JSON object.
171	    """
172	    if not text or not text.strip():
173	        return None, ""
174	    raw = text.strip()
175	    # Extract the first {...} object so a stray code fence / preamble cannot break parse.
176	    match = re.search(r"\{.*\}", raw, re.DOTALL)
177	    if not match:
178	        return None, ""
179	    try:
180	        obj = json.loads(match.group(0))
181	    except (ValueError, TypeError):
182	        return None, ""
183	    if not isinstance(obj, dict):
184	        return None, ""
185	    tier_str = str(obj.get("tier", "")).strip().upper()
186	    if tier_str not in _VALID_TIER_LABELS:
187	        return None, ""
188	    rationale = str(obj.get("rationale", "")).strip()
189	    return TierLevel(tier_str), rationale
190	
191	
192	def llm_tier_one(
193	    signals: ClassificationSignals,
194	    call_llm: Callable[[str], str],
195	) -> ClassificationResult | None:
196	    """Single-source LLM tiering escalation. Returns a ClassificationResult carrying the
197	    LLM tier as a WEIGHT, or ``None`` on judge_error / timeout / malformed output so the
198	    caller keeps the deterministic rules-floor result for that source.
199	
200	    NEVER raises into the caller (LAW II fail-honest): any exception from the injected
201	    ``call_llm`` is captured and degrades to ``None`` (rules-floor fallback).
202	    """
203	    # Rule 0 + Rule 1 parity: a source the deterministic rules-floor EXCLUDES or
204	    # DEGRADES per-row must NEVER be positively re-tiered by the LLM — return
205	    # None so the caller keeps the deterministic floor result (its tier AND its
206	    # exclusion/degradation reasons + ``fetch_degraded`` flag). This covers BOTH:
207	    #   * Rule 0 (retraction, tier_classifier R0_retracted => UNKNOWN): the
208	    #     OpenAlex retraction flag OR an explicit leading retraction / withdrawal
209	    #     marker in the title (the case where the flag is unset because the paper
210	    #     was re-deposited on a preprint host). Without the title leg the LLM
211	    #     could positively tier a title-retracted source.
212	    #   * Rule 1 (T7-stub / ``fetch_degraded``, tier_classifier R1_stub_content_
213	    #     length): a source whose fetched body came back too short. If the LLM
214	    #     re-tiered it, the fresh ``ClassificationResult`` would carry
215	    #     ``fetch_degraded=False`` — LAUNDERING a body-we-could-not-read into a
216	    #     clean positive tier and DROPPING the floor's T7/fetch_degraded signal
217	    #     the adequacy lane relies on to exclude the stub from grounded-content
218	    #     counts. This is the hole the W4 ``__excluded__`` cache key only
219	    #     HALF-closed: the key stops a stub from POOLING into a clean domain, but
220	    #     the excluded stub — as its OWN domain-cache representative, and on the
221	    #     legacy per-row + single-source paths — still ran this LLM override and
222	    #     lost its floor signal. Guarding here closes all three paths at once.
223	    # Keyed on the SAME observable signals the deterministic floor uses
224	    # (``_has_per_row_exclusion_signal``) so the LLM path and the floor can never
225	    # disagree. Faithfulness tightening only (never a positive tier for an
226	    # excluded/degraded source, source still KEPT at its floor WEIGHT — no drop,
227	    # §-1.3); never a relaxation. NOTE: ``content_length == 0`` (metadata-only, no
228	    # body fetched yet) is NOT a stub signal (see ``_has_per_row_exclusion_signal``)
229	    # so not-yet-fetched rows still LLM-tier — breadth preserved.
230	    if _has_per_row_exclusion_signal(signals):
231	        return None
232	    prompt = build_tier_prompt(signals)
233	    try:
234	        text = call_llm(prompt)
235	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-honest: degrade to rules-floor
236	        logger.warning(
237	            "[credibility_llm_tiering] judge_error for %s — falling back to rules-floor: %s",
238	            (signals.url or "")[:80], exc,
239	        )
240	        return None
241	    tier, rationale = parse_tier_response(text)
242	    if tier is None:
243	        logger.warning(
244	            "[credibility_llm_tiering] malformed/out-of-scheme LLM tier for %s — "
245	            "falling back to rules-floor",
246	            (signals.url or "")[:80],
247	        )
248	        return None
249	    return ClassificationResult(
250	        tier=tier,
251	        confidence=0.9,  # LLM-tiering VIEW confidence; not a deterministic 1.0
252	        reasons=[
253	            f"LLM-tiering (PG_CREDIBILITY_LLM_TIERING): assigned {tier.value} — "
254	            f"{rationale or 'no rationale returned'}"
255	        ],
256	        matched_rules=["llm_tiering"],
257	        signals_used={
258	            "url": signals.url,
259	            "title": signals.title,
260	            "publication_type": signals.openalex_publication_type,
261	            "source_type": signals.openalex_source_type,
262	            "venue": signals.openalex_venue,
263	            "content_length": signals.fetched_content_length,
264	        },
265	    )
266	
267	
268	# B2 (#1344) — venue-corroboration backstop (LAW VI kill-switch, default-ON). The GLM can
269	# return a T1/T2 verdict from a bare DOI + scholarly-sounding title even when OpenAlex
270	# resolved NO venue/source_type (the off-topic Russian-cosmetics ev_061 mis-tiered T1/0.95
271	# was exactly this). When the deterministic venue detector cannot corroborate a recognized
272	# peer-reviewed venue, an uncorroborated top-tier (T1/T2) LLM verdict is CAPPED to the
273	# deterministic rules-floor. WEIGHT-only (§-1.3): it can ONLY lower an uncorroborated top
274	# tier; it never raises a tier, never drops a source, never gates release. A genuinely
275	# corroborated journal (host on PEER_REVIEWED_JOURNAL_DOMAINS, peer-reviewed DOI prefix, or
276	# a resolved OpenAlex JOURNAL venue) is untouched. Set
277	# PG_TIER_REQUIRE_VENUE_CORROBORATION=0 to revert to byte-identical legacy behavior.
278	_UNCORROBORATED_TOP_TIERS = frozenset({TierLevel.T1, TierLevel.T2})
279	
280	
281	def _venue_corroboration_required() -> bool:
282	    """LAW VI kill-switch for the B2 venue-corroboration backstop (default-ON)."""
283	    return os.environ.get(
284	        "PG_TIER_REQUIRE_VENUE_CORROBORATION", "1"
285	    ).strip().lower() not in ("0", "false", "no", "off")
286	
287	
288	def _cap_uncorroborated_top_tier(
289	    llm_res: ClassificationResult | None,
290	    signals: ClassificationSignals,
291	    floor_res: ClassificationResult,
292	) -> ClassificationResult | None:
293	    """Return the rules-floor result when the LLM assigned an uncorroborated top tier
294	    (T1/T2) to a source with NO recognized scholarly venue; otherwise the LLM result is
295	    kept unchanged. Only ever LOWERS — never promotes, never drops (§-1.3)."""
296	    if (
297	        llm_res is not None
298	        and _venue_corroboration_required()
299	        # Codex iter-2 P1: the corroboration test uses the STRICT venue-only predicate
300	        # (``_is_corroborated_scholarly_venue``), NOT the widened length-floor helper
301	        # (``_is_known_scholarly_venue``). A bare DOI ("10.") or a lone peer-reviewed flag is
302	        # NOT a recognized venue, so it can no longer slip an uncorroborated T1/T2 LLM verdict
303	        # past this cap (the ev_061 bare-DOI + scholarly-title mis-tier).
304	        and llm_res.tier in _UNCORROBORATED_TOP_TIERS
305	        and not _is_corroborated_scholarly_venue(signals)
306	    ):
307	        return floor_res
308	    return llm_res
309	
310	
311	# ── Statistical-agency tier FLOOR guarantee (PG_WORKFORCE_T3_TARGETING, default-OFF) ──
312	# The LLM-tiering scheme text (_TIER_SCHEME above) describes T3 as CLINICAL government /
313	# regulatory bodies (FDA / EMA / NICE / WHO / CDC / Health Canada) and does NOT name
314	# statistical / data agencies (BLS / OECD / ILO / StatCan / Eurostat / World Bank / IMF).
315	# International orgs like OECD / ILO are not "regulators", so the GLM can DOWN-tier a genuine
316	# statistical-agency page (e.g. OECD Employment Outlook -> T6) below the deterministic
317	# rules-floor's correct T3 (tier_classifier R2b_statistical_agency). Because the LLM tier
318	# OVERRIDES the rules-floor on success, an under-tiered agency page suppresses the workforce

379	    """I-deepfix-001 W07 (#1344): a TOTAL wall (seconds) for the whole LLM-tiering batch.
380	    `classify_sources_llm_tiering` is per-CALL bounded, but `pool.map` BLOCKS until ALL N
381	    futures finish; on a mirror blank-200/trickle storm each source can burn up to its
382	    per-call budget x retries, so the batch wall = ceil(N/workers)*per-call. Because
383	    `run_live_retrieval` runs on the event-loop thread, the run-level wall cannot preempt

388	    try:
389	        value = float(raw)
390	    except (TypeError, ValueError):

481	def _domain_signature(signals: "ClassificationSignals") -> "str | None":
482	    """Cache key for the per-DOMAIN authority weight: normalized domain PLUS the
483	    observable genre signature (publication_type + source_type).
484	
485	    Genre is PART of the key on purpose: two genuinely-different genres on the
486	    SAME host (e.g. a ``nature.com`` peer-reviewed article vs a ``nature.com``
487	    news blog) must still escalate SEPARATELY — the cache only pools rows that
488	    share BOTH domain AND observable genre, so it never launders a blog into a
489	    journal's authority. Returns ``None`` when the url has no resolvable domain;
490	    those rows always escalate individually (never pooled under a blank key)."""

499	def _has_per_row_exclusion_signal(signals: "ClassificationSignals") -> bool:
500	    """True iff this row carries a per-SOURCE exclusion/degradation signal that the
501	    deterministic rules-floor keys on INDIVIDUALLY: retraction (Rule 0 =>
502	    ``R0_retracted`` UNKNOWN, priority-first) or a too-short body (Rule 1 => T7
503	    stub / ``fetch_degraded``).
504	
505	    Such a row must NEVER share the per-domain authority-cache key with clean
506	    same-domain rows. If it were pooled it could (a) INHERIT a clean
507	    representative's positive tier — laundering a retracted or stub source into a
508	    journal's authority, bypassing the Rule-0 parity guard whose whole job is to
509	    keep a retracted source out of any positive tier — or (b), as the
510	    representative, launder its OWN excluded/degraded result onto the whole clean
511	    domain (representative = first row, so a retracted/stub FIRST row is
512	    especially dangerous). Keyed on the SAME observable signals the floor uses, so
513	    the cache decision and the floor decision can never disagree.
514	    """
515	    if signals.openalex_is_retracted:
516	        return True
517	    if _detect_retraction_marker(signals.title or ""):
518	        return True
519	    content_length = signals.fetched_content_length or 0
520	    # 0 == "no body fetched yet" (metadata-only) is NOT a stub signal here; only a
521	    # genuinely short fetched body trips the T7-stub floor.
522	    if 0 < content_length < T7_STUB_CONTENT_CHARS:
523	        return True
524	    return False

527	class TieringBatchResult(list):
528	    """A ``list[ClassificationResult]`` that ALSO carries an honest, machine-readable
529	    ``tiering_status`` dict (I-deepfix-001 D5 #1344).
530	
531	    It IS a list — it iterates, indexes, and ``len()``s EXACTLY like the legacy bare-list
532	    return, so every existing caller (``live_retriever`` zips + indexes it) is byte-
533	    compatible. The extra ``.tiering_status`` attribute lets a downstream surface the
534	    GLM-vs-rules-floor batch MODE into the durable manifest disclosure. Subclassing
535	    ``list`` (NO ``__slots__``) keeps the per-instance ``__dict__`` so the attribute
536	    assignment is legal. ``.tiering_status`` is a machine-readable dict, e.g.
537	    ``{"tiering_mode": "rules_floor_degraded", "llm_success_count": 0,
538	    "rules_floor_count": 200, "fallback_count": 200, "error_count": 0, "total": 200}``."""
539	
540	    def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs) -> None:
541	        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
542	        # PER-INSTANCE default (never a shared class-level mutable); the producer reassigns
543	        # this with the real status before returning.
544	        self.tiering_status: dict = {}

547	def _resolve_tiering_mode(llm_success: int, total: int) -> str:
548	    """Pure: the HONEST batch mode from the REAL runtime counts (never a config echo).
549	
550	    * ``rules_floor_degraded`` — there WERE sources but GLM tiered NONE (llm_success == 0):
551	      every source fell back to the deterministic rules-floor. This is the prior
552	      false-positive the D5 fix kills — it must NEVER be reported as ``tiered_via_glm``.
553	    * ``partial`` — GLM tiered some but not all (0 < llm_success < total).
554	    * ``tiered_via_glm`` — GLM tiered every source (llm_success == total), OR the vacuous
555	      empty-corpus case (total <= 0: nothing to tier, so nothing degraded — the
556	      corpus-zero floor is enforced by separate adequacy gates, not by this weight stage)."""
557	    if total <= 0:
558	        return _TIERING_MODE_TIERED_VIA_GLM
559	    if llm_success <= 0:
560	        return _TIERING_MODE_RULES_FLOOR_DEGRADED
561	    if llm_success >= total:
562	        return _TIERING_MODE_TIERED_VIA_GLM
563	    return _TIERING_MODE_PARTIAL

570	
571	    ``rules_floor_count = total - llm_success`` is the single honest number a preflight /
572	    operator reads: how many of ``total`` sources are carrying the deterministic rules-floor
573	    tier rather than a GLM tier."""
574	    return {
575	        "tiering_mode": _resolve_tiering_mode(llm_success, total),
576	        "llm_success_count": int(llm_success),
577	        "rules_floor_count": int(max(0, total - llm_success)),
578	        "fallback_count": int(fallback_count),
579	        "error_count": int(error_count),
580	        "total": int(total),
581	    }

608	def _run_llm_tiering_serial(
609	    signals_list: list[ClassificationSignals],
610	    caller: Callable[[str], str],
611	) -> tuple[dict[int, "ClassificationResult | None"], int, int, int]:
612	    """WS-13 (#1344): legacy SERIAL execution leg (``PG_TIER_LLM_PARALLEL=0``).
613	
614	    Processes every source in index order with NO ``ThreadPoolExecutor`` and NO batch
615	    wall / circuit-breaker — byte-identical to the pre-parallel serial behavior. Returns
616	    ``(llm_by_idx, llm_success, fallback_count, error_count)``; the shared gather-then-sort
617	    tail in ``classify_sources_llm_tiering`` still applies the deterministic rules-floor
618	    to any ``None`` entry, so no source is ever dropped (§-1.3)."""
619	    llm_by_idx: dict[int, ClassificationResult | None] = {}
620	    llm_success = 0
621	    fallback_count = 0
622	    error_count = 0
623	    for idx in range(len(signals_list)):
624	        _idx, res, status = _tier_one_outcome(signals_list, idx, caller)
625	        llm_by_idx[_idx] = res
626	        if status == _TIER_STATUS_SUCCESS:
627	            llm_success += 1
628	        elif status == _TIER_STATUS_ERROR:
629	            error_count += 1
630	        else:
631	            fallback_count += 1
632	    return llm_by_idx, llm_success, fallback_count, error_count

635	def _run_llm_tiering_parallel(
636	    signals_list: list[ClassificationSignals],
637	    caller: Callable[[str], str],
638	    workers: int,
639	    deadline_monotonic: "float | None",
640	) -> tuple[dict[int, "ClassificationResult | None"], int, int, int]:
641	    """WS-13 (#1344): BOUNDED-PARALLEL execution leg (default via ``PG_TIER_LLM_PARALLEL``).
642	
643	    In-flight requests are capped at ``workers`` (``PG_TIER_LLM_WORKERS``); the whole batch
644	    is bounded by a TOTAL wall (``deadline_monotonic`` and/or ``PG_TIER_LLM_BATCH_WALL_SECONDS``,
645	    tighter wins) plus a consecutive-fallback circuit-breaker. Returns
646	    ``(llm_by_idx, llm_success, fallback_count, error_count)``. Any future un-returned at the
647	    wall (a straggler) is simply absent from ``llm_by_idx`` — the shared tail then keeps that
648	    source's deterministic rules-floor tier (a WEIGHT, no drop, §-1.3)."""
649	    n = len(signals_list)
650	    llm_by_idx: dict[int, ClassificationResult | None] = {}
651	    llm_success = 0
652	    fallback_count = 0
653	    error_count = 0
654	    # I-deepfix-001 W07 (#1344): bound the whole batch by a TOTAL deadline + a
655	    # consecutive-fallback circuit-breaker. The TIGHTER (earlier) of the threaded
656	    # `deadline_monotonic` (the caller's retrieval wall) and the env fallback wins.
657	    _batch_wall = _tier_llm_batch_wall_seconds()
658	    _now_monotonic = time.monotonic()
659	    # I-deepfix-001 (#1344 followup — tiering-starvation fix, Run A 2026-07-02): credibility
660	    # tiering is a CHEAP WEIGHT (T1-T7) whose INCOMPLETENESS silently fabricates a false
661	    # material-deviation corpus — every un-tiered source defaults to the deterministic
662	    # rules-floor, which the downstream corpus_approval gate reads as a T4-dominant skew and
663	    # ABORTS. Run A proved the failure: the retrieval wall had ALREADY been consumed by a slow
664	    # fetch, so ``deadline_monotonic`` arrived here already in the PAST; the old
665	    # ``min(expired, now + batch_wall)`` == the expired instant, so the loop broke on the first
666	    # iteration and tiered ZERO of 20 sources -> false T4-dominant -> abort_corpus_approval_denied.
667	    # Tiering therefore gets its OWN budget from NOW (``_batch_wall``, default 600s), and only
668	    # tightens to the caller's retrieval wall when that wall is STILL IN THE FUTURE. This is
669	    # faithfulness-NEUTRAL: it only changes how many sources receive a real GLM tier vs the
670	    # deterministic rules-floor (a WEIGHT, never a drop — every source is kept either way, §-1.3);
671	    # it never touches strict_verify / NLI / D8 / provenance.
672	    if _batch_wall > 0:
673	        _eff_deadline = _now_monotonic + _batch_wall
674	        if deadline_monotonic is not None and deadline_monotonic > _now_monotonic:
675	            _eff_deadline = min(_eff_deadline, deadline_monotonic)
676	    else:
677	        # Batch wall disabled (``PG_TIER_LLM_BATCH_WALL_SECONDS <= 0``): only a STILL-FUTURE
678	        # retrieval wall bounds the batch; an already-expired one is ignored (never zero-tier).
679	        _eff_deadline = (
680	            deadline_monotonic
681	            if (deadline_monotonic is not None and deadline_monotonic > _now_monotonic)
682	            else None
683	        )
684	    _degrade_after = _tier_llm_degrade_after()
685	    _consecutive_fallbacks = 0
686	    _short_circuited = 0
687	    _wall_unreturned = 0
688	    # I-deepfix-001 W07 (#1344): manage the pool MANUALLY (NOT `with`) so the
689	    # non-blocking shutdown below cannot be defeated by `with`'s __exit__ shutdown(wait=True),
690	    # which would BLOCK until a wedged worker finishes — making the batch wall cosmetic.
691	    pool = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=workers)
692	    try:
693	        future_to_idx = {
694	            pool.submit(_tier_one_outcome, signals_list, i, caller): i
695	            for i in range(n)
696	        }
697	        pending = set(future_to_idx)
698	        _stop = False
699	        # I-fetch-005 (#1344) FIX 4: per-call (per-next-completion) timeout so a SINGLE hung
700	        # tiering call cannot pin the batch for the whole batch wall (~9min observed live). Each
701	        # wait slice is capped at min(remaining batch wall, per-call timeout); a slice that
702	        # elapses with NO completion means the remaining in-flight calls are hung — stop waiting
703	        # and let them fall to the deterministic rules-floor (a WEIGHT, never a drop — §-1.3).
704	        _per_call = _tier_llm_per_call_seconds()
705	        _stall_cutoff = False
706	        while pending and not _stop:
707	            _wall_remaining = (
708	                None if _eff_deadline is None
709	                else max(0.0, _eff_deadline - time.monotonic())
710	            )
711	            if _wall_remaining is not None and _wall_remaining <= 0:
712	                break
713	            # Cap each wait slice at the per-call timeout (when enabled) so the tail is bounded by
714	            # the per-call budget, not the full batch wall.
715	            if _per_call > 0:
716	                _slice = (
717	                    _per_call if _wall_remaining is None
718	                    else min(_wall_remaining, _per_call)
719	                )
720	            else:
721	                _slice = _wall_remaining
722	            done, pending = futures_wait(
723	                pending, timeout=_slice, return_when=FIRST_COMPLETED,
724	            )
725	            if not done:
726	                # No source completed within this slice. If the slice was the PER-CALL cap (there
727	                # was still batch-wall budget left), the remaining pending are hung stragglers —
728	                # cut them off (they keep the rules-floor). Otherwise the batch wall elapsed.
729	                if _per_call > 0 and (
730	                    _wall_remaining is None or _wall_remaining > _slice
731	                ):
732	                    _stall_cutoff = True
733	                break  # batch wall elapsed mid-flight, OR the per-call stall cutoff fired
734	            for fut in done:
735	                idx, res, status = fut.result()
736	                llm_by_idx[idx] = res
737	                if status == _TIER_STATUS_SUCCESS:
738	                    llm_success += 1
739	                    _consecutive_fallbacks = 0
740	                elif status == _TIER_STATUS_ERROR:
741	                    error_count += 1
742	                    _consecutive_fallbacks += 1
743	                else:
744	                    fallback_count += 1
745	                    _consecutive_fallbacks += 1
746	                # Circuit-breaker: once consecutive fallbacks exceed the threshold, stop
747	                # waiting on the rest and let them fall through to the rules-floor.
748	                if _degrade_after > 0 and _consecutive_fallbacks >= _degrade_after:
749	                    _stop = True
750	                    break
751	        # Any future not yet collected (wall hit OR circuit-breaker tripped) keeps the
752	        # deterministic rules-floor (no drop, §-1.3). Do NOT block on the still-running
753	        # futures — they finish in their threads but we stop waiting on them.
754	        if pending:
755	            for fut in list(pending):
756	                if fut.done():
757	                    try:
758	                        idx, res, status = fut.result()
759	                        llm_by_idx[idx] = res
760	                        if status == _TIER_STATUS_SUCCESS:
761	                            llm_success += 1
762	                        elif status == _TIER_STATUS_ERROR:
763	                            error_count += 1
764	                        else:
765	                            fallback_count += 1
766	                        continue
767	                    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — a late failure falls to the floor
768	                        pass
769	                if _stop:
770	                    _short_circuited += 1
771	                else:
772	                    _wall_unreturned += 1
773	        if _short_circuited or _wall_unreturned:
774	            logger.warning(
775	                "[credibility_llm_tiering] W07: batch bounded — %d short-circuited "
776	                "(>=%d consecutive fallbacks), %d un-returned at %s; ALL kept at the "
777	                "deterministic rules-floor (no drop, §-1.3).",
778	                _short_circuited, _degrade_after, _wall_unreturned,
779	                # I-fetch-005 (#1344) FIX 4: distinguish the per-call stall cutoff (a single hung
780	                # call bounded by PG_TIER_LLM_PER_CALL_SECONDS) from the whole-batch wall.
781	                "per-call stall cutoff" if _stall_cutoff else "wall",
782	            )
783	    finally:
784	        # NON-BLOCKING teardown: a wedged tiering worker must not delay the return (the
785	        # un-returned source already keeps its rules-floor). The orphaned thread exits on
786	        # its own per-call timeout.
787	        pool.shutdown(wait=False, cancel_futures=True)
788	    return llm_by_idx, llm_success, fallback_count, error_count
789	
790	
791	def classify_sources_llm_tiering(
792	    signals_list: list[ClassificationSignals],
793	    *,
794	    call_llm: Callable[[str], str] | None = None,
795	    max_workers: int | None = None,
796	    deadline_monotonic: "float | None" = None,
797	    status_out: dict | None = None,
798	    _bypass_domain_cache: bool = False,
799	) -> "TieringBatchResult":
800	    """Bounded-parallel per-SOURCE LLM tiering over a batch of sources.
801	
802	    For EVERY source the deterministic rules-floor is computed first (the instant,
803	    no-network fallback). The LLM escalation then runs bounded-parallel; a source keeps
804	    its rules-floor result on any judge_error / timeout / malformed output. The result
805	    list is order-PRESERVING (gather-then-sort by index) so concurrency never changes
806	    the per-source outcome (§8 determinism invariant). No source is ever dropped
807	    (§-1.3 weight-not-filter) — ``len(result) == len(signals_list)`` ALWAYS.
808	
809	    Returns a ``TieringBatchResult`` — a ``list`` aligned 1:1 with ``signals_list`` that
810	    ALSO carries an honest machine-readable ``.tiering_status`` dict (I-deepfix-001 D5
811	    #1344): ``tiering_mode`` in {``tiered_via_glm``, ``partial``, ``rules_floor_degraded``}
812	    plus ``llm_success_count`` / ``rules_floor_count`` / ``total``. A 100%-rules-floor batch
813	    (every GLM call errored on a blank-200 / trickle storm) is reported as
814	    ``rules_floor_degraded``, NEVER as ``tiered_via_glm``, and emits a LOUD warning — the
815	    run still CONTINUES (credibility is a WEIGHT, not a gate), the degrade is just DISCLOSED.
816	    ``status_out`` (optional): if a dict is passed it is updated in place with the SAME
817	    status, an explicit channel for callers that cannot read the return attribute.
818	    """
819	    n = len(signals_list)
820	    if n == 0:
821	        empty = TieringBatchResult([])
822	        empty.tiering_status = _build_tiering_status(
823	            llm_success=0, fallback_count=0, error_count=0, total=0,
824	        )
825	        if status_out is not None:
826	            status_out.update(empty.tiering_status)
827	        return empty
828	    # W4 (I-deepfix-001): per-DOMAIN authority-weight cache. When enabled, tier ONE
829	    # representative per (domain + genre signature) via GLM and propagate the
830	    # domain-level authority WEIGHT to the rest, so a large corpus with repeated
831	    # domains pays the LLM at most once per domain-genre (never blowing the wall on
832	    # the biggest corpora). No source is dropped (§-1.3). Default-OFF => this branch
833	    # is skipped entirely and the legacy per-row fan-out below runs byte-identical.
834	    if _domain_authority_cache_enabled() and not _bypass_domain_cache:
835	        return _classify_with_domain_authority_cache(
836	            signals_list,
837	            call_llm=call_llm,
838	            max_workers=max_workers,
839	            deadline_monotonic=deadline_monotonic,
840	            status_out=status_out,
841	        )
842	    # Deterministic floor for every source first — the instant fallback (no network).
843	    floor_results: list[ClassificationResult] = [
844	        _classify_source_tier_rules(s) for s in signals_list
845	    ]
846	    caller = call_llm if call_llm is not None else _default_caller()
847	    workers = max_workers if max_workers is not None else _tier_llm_workers()
848	
849	    # WS-13 (#1344): pick the execution leg via the LAW VI kill-switch. Default-ON
850	    # runs the BOUNDED-PARALLEL leg (in-flight capped at ``workers`` = PG_TIER_LLM_WORKERS,
851	    # bounded by the batch wall + circuit-breaker) so the fetched sources get tiered inside
852	    # the retrieval wall instead of being dropped unclassified. OFF runs the legacy SERIAL
853	    # leg (byte-identical to the pre-parallel behavior). BOTH legs feed the SAME deterministic
854	    # gather-then-sort tail below, so switching legs never changes a per-source classification
855	    # — only throughput. Neither leg ever drops a source (rules-floor fallback in the tail).
856	    if _tier_llm_parallel_enabled():
857	        llm_by_idx, llm_success, fallback_count, error_count = _run_llm_tiering_parallel(
858	            signals_list, caller, workers, deadline_monotonic,
859	        )
860	    else:
861	        llm_by_idx, llm_success, fallback_count, error_count = _run_llm_tiering_serial(
862	            signals_list, caller,
863	        )
864	
865	    # Gather-then-sort: walk indices in order, prefer the LLM tier, fall back to floor.
866	    out: list[ClassificationResult] = []
867	    # I-deepfix-001 Wave-9 (#1344) ANTI-DARK activation telemetry (LOGGING-ONLY, faithfulness-neutral):
868	    # count the known statistical-agency sources this batch RAISED to their deterministic rules-floor
869	    # tier — the realized effect of PG_WORKFORCE_T3_TARGETING. Pure observation of the existing floor
870	    # decision below; changes no tier, no drop/keep, no faithfulness path.
871	    _workforce_t3_promoted = 0
872	    for idx in range(n):
873	        llm_res = llm_by_idx.get(idx)
874	        # B2 (#1344): cap an uncorroborated top-tier (T1/T2) GLM verdict to the
875	        # deterministic rules-floor when no recognized scholarly venue corroborates it.
876	        chosen = _cap_uncorroborated_top_tier(
877	            llm_res, signals_list[idx], floor_results[idx],
878	        )
879	        if llm_res is not None and chosen is not llm_res:
880	            logger.warning(
881	                "[credibility_llm_tiering] B2 venue-corroboration CAP: GLM tier %s -> "
882	                "floor %s for %s (no recognized peer-reviewed venue; "
883	                "PG_TIER_REQUIRE_VENUE_CORROBORATION). WEIGHT lowered, source NOT "
884	                "dropped (§-1.3).",
885	                llm_res.tier.value, chosen.tier.value,
886	                (signals_list[idx].url or "")[:80],
887	            )
888	        # PG_WORKFORCE_T3_TARGETING statistical-agency FLOOR: raise an under-tiered
889	        # KNOWN statistical-agency source back UP to its deterministic rules-floor tier
890	        # (never drop, never lower a higher LLM tier — §-1.3). Default-OFF => no-op.
891	        _pre_agency = chosen
892	        chosen = _floor_known_statistical_agency(
893	            chosen, signals_list[idx], floor_results[idx],
894	        )
895	        if chosen is not _pre_agency and chosen is not None:
896	            _workforce_t3_promoted += 1
897	            logger.warning(
898	                "[credibility_llm_tiering] statistical-agency FLOOR: raised tier %s -> "
899	                "%s for %s (known statistical/data agency; PG_WORKFORCE_T3_TARGETING). "
900	                "WEIGHT raised to the deterministic floor, source NOT dropped (§-1.3).",
901	                (_pre_agency.tier.value if _pre_agency is not None else "floor"),
902	                chosen.tier.value,
903	                (signals_list[idx].url or "")[:80],
904	            )
905	        out.append(chosen if chosen is not None else floor_results[idx])
906	
907	    # I-deepfix-001 Wave-9 (#1344) ANTI-DARK activation marker (LOGGING-ONLY): when
908	    # PG_WORKFORCE_T3_TARGETING is ON, surface the HONEST realized count of sources this batch raised to
909	    # the statistical-agency floor tier so a FORCE_ON'd-but-DARK flag can no longer pass the run_gate_b
910	    # activation canary. ``promoted=0`` (this corpus carried no under-tiered statistical/data agency) is an
911	    # honest ran-ok-zero the canary ACCEPTS (§-1.3 — never a >0 gate). OFF => no marker (byte-identical).
912	    # Flag read ONCE here at emit time (LAW VI). No fail/degrade branch — the floor is a pure per-source
913	    # decision, so there is no ``unavailable_failopen`` twin.
914	    if _workforce_t3_targeting_enabled():
915	        logger.info(
916	            "[activation] workforce_t3_targeting: promoted=%d checked=%d",
917	            _workforce_t3_promoted, n,
918	        )
919	
920	    # POST-execution canary + HONEST machine-readable status (D5 #1344) — REAL runtime
921	    # counts, fired only after the fan-out ran (NEVER a config echo). ``len(out) == n``
922	    # always (no source dropped — tier is a WEIGHT, §-1.3). When GLM tiered NOTHING the
923	    # batch is ``rules_floor_degraded``; it is NEVER falsely reported as ``tiered_via_glm``
924	    # (the prior false-positive). The run CONTINUES — credibility is a weight, not a gate —
925	    # but the degrade is DISCLOSED via a LOUD warning + the status carried on the result.
926	    status = _build_tiering_status(
927	        llm_success=llm_success, fallback_count=fallback_count,
928	        error_count=error_count, total=n,
929	    )
930	    mode = status["tiering_mode"]
931	    if mode == _TIERING_MODE_RULES_FLOOR_DEGRADED:
932	        # LOUD disclosure (§-1.3). The literal "DEGRADED (rules-floor only)" + "GLM tiering did
933	        # NOT fire" substrings are the post-run W8 firing-marker FORBID twins
934	        # (scripts/dr_benchmark/run_gate_b.py:2010) — preserved VERBATIM so the post-run audit
935	        # still detects the degrade; the new ``tiering_mode=rules_floor_degraded`` field is the
936	        # machine-readable disclosure (also carried on the returned result + manifest status).
937	        logger.warning(
938	            "[credibility_llm_tiering] DEGRADED (rules-floor only): attempted=%d llm_success=0 "
939	            "fallback=%d error=%d — GLM tiering did NOT fire (blank-200/trickle storm?); "
940	            "tiering_mode=rules_floor_degraded — ALL %d sources kept at the deterministic "
941	            "rules-floor (WEIGHT, no drop, §-1.3). DISCLOSED, never a false 'tiered_via_glm' claim.",
942	            n, fallback_count, error_count, n,
943	        )
944	    else:
945	        # GLM tiered at least one source. The literal "[credibility_llm_tiering] tiered via GLM"
946	        # substring is the post-run W8 firing-marker must_contain (run_gate_b.py:2011) — preserved
947	        # VERBATIM so the audit detects the genuine fire on BOTH the full and partial paths (legacy
948	        # parity: it fired for any llm_success>0). ``tiering_mode=`` now HONESTLY distinguishes a
949	        # full (tiered_via_glm) batch from a partial one. A partial batch logs LOUD (WARNING) since
950	        # some sources fell back to the rules-floor; a full batch logs INFO.
951	        _fire_line = (
952	            "[credibility_llm_tiering] tiered via GLM: attempted=%d llm_success=%d fallback=%d "
953	            "error=%d (tiering_mode=%s rules_floor=%d; WEIGHT, no drop, §-1.3)"
954	        )
955	        _fire_args = (n, llm_success, fallback_count, error_count, mode, n - llm_success)
956	        if mode == _TIERING_MODE_PARTIAL:
957	            logger.warning(_fire_line, *_fire_args)
958	        else:
959	            logger.info(_fire_line, *_fire_args)
960	
961	    result = TieringBatchResult(out)
962	    result.tiering_status = status
963	    if status_out is not None:
964	        status_out.update(status)
965	    return result
966	
967	
968	def _classify_with_domain_authority_cache(
969	    signals_list: list[ClassificationSignals],
970	    *,
971	    call_llm: Callable[[str], str] | None,
972	    max_workers: int | None,
973	    deadline_monotonic: "float | None",
974	    status_out: dict | None,
975	) -> "TieringBatchResult":
976	    """W4 (I-deepfix-001): tier ONE representative per (domain + genre signature)
977	    via GLM and PROPAGATE the domain-level authority WEIGHT to every row sharing
978	    the signature.
979	
980	    Contract preserved from ``classify_sources_llm_tiering``:
981	      * ``len(result) == len(signals_list)`` ALWAYS — no source is ever dropped
982	        (§-1.3 weight-not-filter).
983	      * order-PRESERVING: ``result[i]`` is the tier for ``signals_list[i]``.
984	      * rows with no resolvable domain (signature ``None``) are given a UNIQUE key
985	        so they are NEVER pooled — each escalates individually.
986	      * rows carrying a per-SOURCE exclusion/degradation signal (retracted, or a
987	        T7-stub short body — see ``_has_per_row_exclusion_signal``) are ALSO given
988	        a unique key so they escalate individually: a retracted/stub source can
989	        never inherit a clean domain's tier, and can never (as a representative)
990	        launder its excluded result onto the clean rows of that domain.
991	    A propagated (non-representative) row carries a ``domain_authority_cache_
992	    propagated:<domain>`` reason so the audit trail shows the WEIGHT came from the
993	    domain cache (never a silent copy). The returned ``tiering_status`` is the
994	    representative batch's honest status, annotated with the cache counts.
995	    """
996	    n = len(signals_list)
997	    # Group by domain-genre signature; rows with no domain — OR carrying a per-row
998	    # exclusion/degradation signal (retracted / T7-stub) — get a unique per-index
999	    # key so they escalate individually and are never pooled (Fable P1: a retracted
1000	    # or stub source must never inherit a clean domain's tier, and must never
1001	    # launder its own excluded result onto the clean domain as representative).
1002	    sig_of: list[str] = []
1003	    first_index_of_sig: dict[str, int] = {}
1004	    for i, s in enumerate(signals_list):
1005	        sig = _domain_signature(s)
1006	        if sig is None:
1007	            sig = f"__nodomain__{i}"
1008	        elif _has_per_row_exclusion_signal(s):
1009	            sig = f"__excluded__{i}"
1010	        sig_of.append(sig)
1011	        if sig not in first_index_of_sig:
1012	            first_index_of_sig[sig] = i
1013	
1014	    rep_indices = list(first_index_of_sig.values())
1015	    rep_signals = [signals_list[i] for i in rep_indices]
1016	
1017	    # Tier ONLY the representatives (bypass the cache to avoid infinite recursion).
1018	    # This reuses the FULL legacy path — rules-floor, B2 venue-corroboration cap,
1019	    # statistical-agency floor, wall + breaker — for the representative batch.
1020	    rep_result = classify_sources_llm_tiering(
1021	        rep_signals,
1022	        call_llm=call_llm,
1023	        max_workers=max_workers,
1024	        deadline_monotonic=deadline_monotonic,
1025	        _bypass_domain_cache=True,
1026	    )
1027	    rep_by_sig: dict[str, ClassificationResult] = {}
1028	    for local_idx, global_idx in enumerate(rep_indices):
1029	        rep_by_sig[sig_of[global_idx]] = rep_result[local_idx]
1030	
1031	    out: list[ClassificationResult] = []
1032	    for i in range(n):
1033	        sig = sig_of[i]
1034	        rep = rep_by_sig[sig]
1035	        if i == first_index_of_sig[sig]:
1036	            out.append(rep)  # the representative itself
1037	        else:
1038	            domain = sig.split("|", 1)[0]
1039	            out.append(
1040	                dataclasses.replace(
1041	                    rep,
1042	                    reasons=list(rep.reasons)
1043	                    + [f"domain_authority_cache_propagated:{domain}"],
1044	                )
1045	            )
1046	
1047	    result = TieringBatchResult(out)
1048	    status = dict(getattr(rep_result, "tiering_status", None) or {})
1049	    # Honest cache disclosure: how many rows were served from how many GLM-tiered
1050	    # domain signatures. ``rep_status`` counts are over the representative batch;
1051	    # these extra fields expose the fan-out so a preflight/operator can read it.
1052	    status["domain_authority_cache"] = True
1053	    status["unique_domain_signatures"] = len(first_index_of_sig)
1054	    status["total_rows"] = n
1055	    result.tiering_status = status
1056	    if status_out is not None:
1057	        status_out.update(status)
1058	    logger.info(
1059	        "[credibility_llm_tiering] W4 domain-authority cache: %d rows served from "
1060	        "%d unique domain-genre signatures (GLM paid once per signature; WEIGHT "
1061	        "propagated, no drop, §-1.3).",
1062	        n, len(first_index_of_sig),
1063	    )
1064	    return result
1065	
1066	
1067	def classify_source_tier_llm(signals: ClassificationSignals) -> ClassificationResult:
1068	    """Single-source ON-path entry called by ``classify_source_tier`` when the flag is
1069	    ON. Escalates to the LLM, falling back to the rules-floor on any error. The
1070	    bounded-parallel batch path (``classify_sources_llm_tiering``) is the preferred
1071	    high-throughput entry; this keeps the single-source dispatcher contract intact."""
1072	    floor = _classify_source_tier_rules(signals)
1073	    llm_res = llm_tier_one(signals, _default_caller())
1074	    # I-deepfix-002 (#1363): apply the SAME B2 uncorroborated-top-tier cap as the batch
1075	    # path so the single-source dispatcher cannot return an uncorroborated T1/T2 from a
1076	    # bare DOI/title either (gated by PG_TIER_REQUIRE_VENUE_CORROBORATION; only lowers).
1077	    capped = _cap_uncorroborated_top_tier(llm_res, signals, floor)
1078	    # PG_WORKFORCE_T3_TARGETING statistical-agency FLOOR (default-OFF no-op): keep the
1079	    # single-source dispatcher aligned with the batch path so a known statistical-agency
1080	    # source is not left under-tiered here either (only ever raises to the floor, §-1.3).
1081	    floored = _floor_known_statistical_agency(capped, signals, floor)
1082	    return floored if floored is not None else floor
1083

1	"""Sweep-side adapter for the citation-snowball evidence_deepener (I-meta-002-q1d #942-deepener).
2	
3	Wires POLARIS's frontier-grade `evidence_deepener` (backward+forward S2 citation chase + recommendations
4	+ mechanism search) into the launch sweep behind a flag + a Stop-RAG value-based trigger. SAFETY DESIGN
5	(Codex brief-gate, Option A): the deepener only DISCOVERS relevant primary-paper URLs; those URLs are then
6	fed back through the EXISTING `run_live_retrieval(seed_urls=...)` chokepoint (fetch → classify_source_tier
7	→ is_content_starved → _build_provenance_quote), so a deepened paper earns its tier ONLY from fetched
8	content and a thin/abstract-only paper is DROPPED fail-closed — no tier laundering, zero new evidence-row
9	build logic that could drift from strict_verify.
10	
11	Pure orchestration helpers (no I/O except the injected `deepen_evidence` coroutine). The sweep does the
12	merge + the `run_live_retrieval` pass; this module only builds the deepener state, runs the coroutine
13	safely from a sync caller, applies the trigger predicate, and extracts the discovered URLs.
14	"""
15	
16	from __future__ import annotations
17	
18	import asyncio
19	import concurrent.futures
20	import os
21	from typing import Any, Awaitable, Callable
22	
23	# Model the deepener uses for its mechanism-query generation LLM call (env-overridable).
24	DEEPENER_LLM_MODEL = os.getenv("PG_SWEEP_DEEPENER_MODEL", "deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro")
25	
26	# Default URL injection cap (bounds the deepener fetch pass; also env-overridable at the call site).
27	DEFAULT_DEEPENER_URL_CAP = 20
28	
29	
30	def should_trigger_deepener(
31	    *,
32	    flag_on: bool,
33	    has_s2_key: bool,
34	    has_seed_evidence: bool,
35	    adequacy_decision: str,
36	    total_uncovered: int,
37	    corpus_review_heavy: bool = False,
38	) -> bool:
39	    """Stop-RAG value-based trigger (Codex brief-gate iter-1 required predicate). The deepener fires
40	    ONLY when it is enabled AND there is something to chase from AND the corpus is BORDERLINE — i.e.
41	    NOT on an already-comfortably-adequate corpus with full coverage.
42	
43	    - `flag_on`: PG_SWEEP_EVIDENCE_DEEPENER truthy (the deepener SPENDS).
44	    - `has_s2_key`: SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_API_KEY present (the deepener no-ops without it).
45	    - `has_seed_evidence`: there is existing evidence to chase citations FROM (else "abort-impossible").
46	    - borderline := adequacy.decision != "proceed" (i.e. "expand"/"abort") OR post-R6 uncovered topics > 0.
47	    - `corpus_review_heavy` (R1_deepener_enable): ALSO deepen a corpus that is comfortably adequate +
48	      fully covered but REVIEW-HEAVY / PRIMARY-STARVED — the task72 blocked-reference miss (a systematic
49	      review COVERS the topic so adequacy='proceed' + uncovered==0, but the primary studies behind it
50	      were never fetched). Computed by is_review_heavy_or_primary_starved. Default False keeps the
51	      pre-R1 behaviour byte-identical. This ONLY widens WHEN the snowball runs; every discovered URL
52	      still earns its tier through the unchanged fetch/tier/strict_verify chokepoint (§-1.3 widen-only).
53	
54	    The flag / key / seed-evidence preconditions are HARD — corpus_review_heavy never bypasses them.
55	
56	    FAIL-LOUD (LAW II, I-deepfix-001 #1344 wiring-gap iter-4, Codex REVISE): this predicate is THE single
57	    chokepoint EVERY real paid sweep entry flows through — run_gate_b.main(), run_gate_b.run_gate_b_query(),
58	    AND scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.run_one_query() (the main_async/main path that bypasses run_gate_b) all
59	    gate the citation-snowball deepener on this call. So when the deepener is EXPLICITLY enabled
60	    (PG_SWEEP_EVIDENCE_DEEPENER truthy -> `flag_on`) but the Semantic Scholar key is missing/empty
61	    (`has_s2_key` False), RAISE a clear RuntimeError naming SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_API_KEY INSTEAD of silently
62	    returning False. A silent False would leave the recall lever DARK on a paid run (the deepener no-ops
63	    without the key — evidence_deepener returns {}); this one guard covers all entries. FAITHFULNESS-NEUTRAL:
64	    an env-derived precondition check only — no strict_verify / NLI / 4-role D8 / provenance / span-grounding
65	    logic is touched. The raise fires ONLY on flag-on + key-absent; EVERY other non-trigger reason (flag off,
66	    no seed evidence, adequacy 'proceed' + fully covered + not review-heavy) still returns False WITHOUT
67	    raising, and a hermetic / fake-transport sweep test that never calls this predicate (run_one_query mocked)
68	    never trips it.
69	    """
70	    if flag_on and not has_s2_key:
71	        raise RuntimeError(
72	            "PG_SWEEP_EVIDENCE_DEEPENER is ON but SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_API_KEY is missing/empty — the "
73	            "citation-snowball evidence deepener (the recall lever for blocked-reference / "
74	            "primary-starved corpora) would silently NO-OP, leaving backward+forward primary-study "
75	            "chasing DARK on a paid run (LAW II fail-loud). Export SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_API_KEY to enable "
76	            "the deepener, or set PG_SWEEP_EVIDENCE_DEEPENER=0 to disable it explicitly."
77	        )
78	    if not (flag_on and has_s2_key and has_seed_evidence):
79	        return False
80	    return adequacy_decision != "proceed" or total_uncovered > 0 or corpus_review_heavy
81	
82	
83	# R1_deepener_enable: systematic-review / meta-analysis title markers. Mirrors the canonical
84	# tier_classifier._detect_systematic_review_from_title keyword set, inlined so this pure-orchestration
85	# adapter stays import-light + dependency-free (module docstring). Used ONLY to DECIDE whether to widen
86	# retrieval — never to filter, demote, or re-tier a source (§-1.3 WEIGHT-don't-FILTER).
87	_SR_MA_TITLE_MARKERS = (
88	    "systematic review",
89	    "meta-analysis",
90	    "meta analysis",
91	    "network meta-analysis",
92	    "cochrane review",
93	    "umbrella review",
94	    "scoping review",
95	    "pooled analysis",
96	)
97	
98	
99	def _title_is_systematic_review(title: str) -> bool:
100	    if not title:
101	        return False
102	    t = title.lower()
103	    return any(k in t for k in _SR_MA_TITLE_MARKERS)
104	
105	
106	def is_review_heavy_or_primary_starved(
107	    *,
108	    classified_sources: list[Any] | None,
109	    tier_counts: dict[str, int] | None,
110	) -> bool:
111	    """R1_deepener_enable AUTO-TRIGGER — fire the citation-snowball deepener on the blocked-reference /
112	    primary-starved corpus the value-based borderline gate (should_trigger_deepener) misses.
113	
114	    task72 pathology (proven from the Box-B drb_72 corpus): a systematic review COVERS the topic so
115	    corpus_adequacy='proceed' AND completeness.total_uncovered==0, yet T1+T2 = 14/182 (7.7%) — the
116	    primary studies behind a (blocked) systematic review were never fetched.
117	
118	    WIDEN-ONLY (§-1.3): this predicate ONLY decides WHETHER to run the snowball. Every URL the deepener
119	    discovers is still fed back through the UNCHANGED run_live_retrieval(seed_urls=...) -> fetch ->
120	    classify_source_tier -> is_content_starved -> strict_verify chokepoint; a thin/abstract-only paper
121	    is DROPPED fail-closed and the FROZEN faithfulness engine re-grounds every claim. Nothing here
122	    filters, demotes, caps, or auto-trusts a source.
123	
124	    Two OR'd signals, both env-tunable (LAW VI):
125	      (A) review-heavy-with-thin-primaries := (>= PG_DEEPENER_REVIEW_MIN_COUNT systematic reviews /
126	          meta-analyses present — T2 tier OR title-detected SR/MA, catching a review mis-tiered off T2)
127	          AND (T1 primary fraction < PG_DEEPENER_REVIEW_PRIMARY_CEIL). A review exists but the primaries
128	          behind it are missing — the blocked-reference signature.
129	      (B) primary-starved := (T1+T2) fraction of the corpus < PG_DEEPENER_PRIMARY_STARVED_FRAC — the
130	          top peer-reviewed evidence is thin overall.
131	    """
132	    counts = dict(tier_counts or {})
133	    total = 0
134	    for v in counts.values():
135	        try:
136	            total += int(v)
137	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
138	            continue
139	    sources = list(classified_sources or [])
140	    if total <= 0:
141	        total = len(sources)
142	    if total <= 0:
143	        return False
144	
145	    def _tier_count(key: str) -> int:
146	        try:
147	            return int(counts.get(key, 0) or 0)
148	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
149	            return 0
150	
151	    t1 = _tier_count("T1")
152	    t2 = _tier_count("T2")
153	
154	    # Title-detected SR/MA among sources NOT already counted as T2 — a review mis-tiered as T4/T6/T7
155	    # (e.g. the blocked sciencedirect systematic review on task72). Additive to the T2 review count.
156	    review_titles = 0
157	    for s in sources:
158	        if isinstance(s, dict):
159	            tier = str(s.get("tier", "") or "")
160	            title = str(s.get("title", "") or "")
161	        else:
162	            tier = str(getattr(s, "tier", "") or "")
163	            title = str(getattr(s, "title", "") or "")
164	        if tier.upper() != "T2" and _title_is_systematic_review(title):
165	            review_titles += 1
166	
167	    review_count = t2 + review_titles
168	    primary_frac = t1 / total
169	    top_tier_frac = (t1 + t2) / total
170	
171	    def _env_int(name: str, default: int) -> int:
172	        try:
173	            return int(os.getenv(name, str(default)))
174	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
175	            return default
176	
177	    def _env_float(name: str, default: float) -> float:
178	        try:
179	            return float(os.getenv(name, str(default)))
180	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
181	            return default
182	
183	    review_min = _env_int("PG_DEEPENER_REVIEW_MIN_COUNT", 1)
184	    review_primary_ceil = _env_float("PG_DEEPENER_REVIEW_PRIMARY_CEIL", 0.40)
185	    primary_starved_frac = _env_float("PG_DEEPENER_PRIMARY_STARVED_FRAC", 0.25)
186	
187	    review_heavy = (review_count >= review_min) and (primary_frac < review_primary_ceil)
188	    primary_starved = top_tier_frac < primary_starved_frac
189	    return review_heavy or primary_starved
190	
191	
192	def build_deepener_state(evidence_rows: list[dict[str, Any]], question: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
193	    """Build the 3-key state the deepener reads (`iteration_count`, `evidence` carrying `source_url`,
194	    `original_query`). Pure."""
195	    evidence = []
196	    for ev in evidence_rows or []:
197	        # Strip BEFORE the blank check (Codex diff-gate iter-1 P1): a whitespace-only url is not seed
198	        # evidence.
199	        url = (ev.get("source_url") or ev.get("url") or "").strip()
200	        if url:
201	            evidence.append({"source_url": url})
202	    return {"iteration_count": 0, "evidence": evidence, "original_query": question or ""}
203	
204	
205	def run_deepener_sync(
206	    state: dict[str, Any],
207	    *,
208	    deepen_fn: Callable[[dict[str, Any]], Awaitable[dict[str, Any]]] | None = None,
209	) -> dict[str, Any]:
210	    """Run the async `deepen_evidence` coroutine from a SYNC caller WITHOUT raising RuntimeError if an
211	    event loop is already running (Codex brief-gate iter-1 P1). Normal sweep execution has no running
212	    loop → `asyncio.run`. If a loop IS running (async test harness / embedded caller), run the coroutine
213	    in an ISOLATED thread with its own loop so it fails closed locally and never aborts unrelated work.
214	
215	    `deepen_fn` is injectable so offline tests pass a FAKE deepener (no network/spend). The REAL
216	    `deepen_evidence(client, state)` takes an OpenRouterClient FIRST (Codex diff-gate iter-2 P1) — the
217	    default closure constructs one and adapts it to the 1-arg `fn(state)` interface used here.
218	    """
219	    if deepen_fn is None:
220	        from src.polaris_graph.agents.evidence_deepener import deepen_evidence  # noqa: E402
221	        from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import OpenRouterClient  # noqa: E402
222	
223	        async def deepen_fn(_state: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
224	            # Close the client after the pass to avoid leaking its async HTTP client (Codex diff-gate
225	            # iter-3 P2; mirrors the generator pattern, e.g. analyst_synthesis).
226	            client = OpenRouterClient(model=DEEPENER_LLM_MODEL)
227	            try:
228	                return await deepen_evidence(client, _state)
229	            finally:
230	                await client.close()
231	
232	    try:
233	        asyncio.get_running_loop()
234	        loop_running = True
235	    except RuntimeError:
236	        loop_running = False
237	
238	    if loop_running:
239	        with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=1) as executor:
240	            return executor.submit(lambda: asyncio.run(deepen_fn(state))).result()
241	    return asyncio.run(deepen_fn(state))
242	
243	
244	def discovered_urls(deepener_output: dict[str, Any] | None, *, cap: int = DEFAULT_DEEPENER_URL_CAP) -> list[str]:
245	    """Extract de-duplicated, non-blank discovered paper URLs from the deepener output, capped. These
246	    are seeded into the `run_live_retrieval(seed_urls=...)` chokepoint (not turned into evidence rows
247	    here — the existing fetch/tier/provenance pipeline does that)."""
248	    # Exact cap (Codex diff-gate iter-1 P1): a non-positive cap yields NO urls (and never flows a
249	    # negative into fetch_cap) — guard up front so the append-then-break can't return one url at cap=0.
250	    if cap is None or cap <= 0:
251	        return []
252	    papers = (deepener_output or {}).get("deepened_papers", []) or []
253	    out: list[str] = []
254	    seen: set[str] = set()
255	    for paper in papers:
256	        url = (paper.get("url") or "").strip() if isinstance(paper, dict) else ""
257	        if not url or url in seen:
258	            continue
259	        seen.add(url)
260	        out.append(url)
261	        if len(out) >= cap:
262	            break
263	    return out
